summary: Merged a large ftCommon matching pass in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c. Despite the file/title, the touched code mostly covers item-scope states and common catch/capture/tether behavior, plus header prototype cleanup. decomp.dev reported 33 new matches and +5844 matched bytes (+0.15%), while also flagging 3 broken matches in the same unit. Review centered on removing empty or unbalanced #pragma ranges and avoiding inline assembly outside SDK code.

key_files.role: Main implementation file; 888 additions/85 deletions. Added many C bodies for item-scope, catch, Link/Samus tether/grapple cleanup, and capture wait/pulled/damage helpers.
Header cleanup; 6 prototype updates replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with typed signatures such as bool ftCo_800D72A0(Fighter*) and void fn_800D7938(Fighter_GObj*).

what_changed: Implemented many previously placeholder-marked functions in ftCo_Attack100.c, including item scope start/air-start transitions, rapid-fire animations, fire/end transitions, and callbacks such as ft_800D76B8, ft_800D7770, fn_800D7830, fn_800D78B0, fn_800D80F4, fn_800D8140, fn_800D8268, fn_800D82EC, fn_800D874C, fn_800D87C0, and fn_800D8838.

Added catch/capture logic for common fighter states: ftCo_800D8AE0 and ftCo_800D8B9C catch-dash checks, fn_800D9228 Link/CLINK hookshot progression, fn_800D949C and ftCo_800D94D8 hookshot teardown, fn_800D9C64 Samus grapple cleanup, and ftCo_CatchWait_IASA item-specific cleanup paths.

Added capture math and state helpers including ftCo_800DA824, fn_800DAC78, fn_800DAEEC, ftCo_CapturePulledLw_Phys, fn_800DB230, ftCo_800DB368, ftCo_800DB500, fn_800DB790, fn_800DBAE4, fn_800DBBF8, ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys, fn_800DBED4, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, and fn_800DC624.

Introduced local padded structs and overlay casts for interim layout access, including FighterOverlay, ftCo_SamusCatchAttrs, ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs, and local ItemScopeVars at offsets around x2340/x2344.
Added includes needed for the matched logic, including ftCommon/types.h, itlinkhookshot.h, it/inlines.h, mp/mplib.h, baselib gobj/jobj/random, and ftLink init support.

decomp.dev automated report recorded 33 new matches and 4 improvements in unmatched items, but also showed broken matches for fn_800DC624, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, and ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll; the slice does not provide reviewer discussion of those bot-reported regressions.
smart_moves: Kept per-character item behavior guarded by fighter kind checks: Link/CLINK hookshot paths, Samus grapple cleanup, and Yoshi-specific capture hurtbox/intangible setup are separated instead of generalized prematurely.

Matched item/fighter lifecycle details, not just motion IDs: the code wires take_dmg_cb, accessory callbacks, death callbacks, hookshot item creation/destruction, and ftLk_Init_BoomerangExists cleanup.
Used small padded local structs to expose only known dat_attrs and overlay offsets needed for matching, avoiding an unsupported full struct-layout claim.

Factored repeated capture transition sequences into static inline helpers such as fn_800DB230_inline, ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys_inline, and fn_800DBED4_inline, preserving repeated codegen patterns while keeping C readable.
After review, the final approach uses C plus #pragma dont_inline and stack/register tactics instead of leaving an inline assembly implementation.
decomp_lessons: Do not leave empty #pragma push/pop or #pragma dont_inline ranges in matching diffs; reviewers explicitly called out empty ranges and unbalanced push/pop pairs.
Inline assembly is discouraged for non-SDK code in the current objdiff workflow; reviewer guidance was to avoid it even if it used to be common before whole-file objdiff support.
When a body is decompiled, update the header in the same PR so UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS do not outlive the matched implementation.
For common fighter item/capture routines, matching often depends on callback assignments and item teardown side effects as much as the Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls.
Padded interim structs are useful, but they should be treated as evidence-limited offset carriers; future work can rename fields like x94/x98/x9C/xA0/xBC only when semantics are confirmed.
The PR body notes model-generated temp variable names were more common than desired; future AI-assisted decomp passes should clean temp_* names where the role is clear, but avoid inventing semantics.
naming_conventions: Common fighter symbols use the ftCo_ prefix; unnamed/internal routines remain address-based fn_800Dxxxx names until better names are proven.
Header prototypes preserve address comments such as /* 0D7938 */ alongside the typed signature.
Typical local names in the diff include Fighter_GObj* gobj, Fighter* fp, FtMotionId msid, Item_GObj* tether_gobj, and Item* tether_ip.
Interim layout structs are named by subsystem/fighter, such as ftCo_SamusCatchAttrs and ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs, with padding fields pad_0/pad_A4 and unknown offset fields x94/x98/x9C/xA0/xBC.
Author explicitly disliked the abundance of temp variable names from the model output; no reviewer-enforced naming rule beyond that is present in the slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop around stubborn functions, with review requiring balanced ranges.
Used PAD_STACK(4/8), volatile padding arrays, and UNUSED volatile padding to influence stack layout.
Used register locals such as register Fighter* temp_r31 and register Fighter_GObj* temp_r30 in some capture functions to steer register allocation.
Used static inline helper wrappers to match inlining boundaries while keeping callable public/static functions.
Used old-style K&R parameter syntax for fn_800DBED4 in the final diff, likely as a codegen tactic.
Used extern int symbols cast through *(f32*) for float constants such as ftCo_804D9020, ftCo_804D9024, ftCo_804D9028, ftCo_804D9030, ftCo_804D9034, ftCo_804D9038, and ftCo_804D903C.
Reviewer guidance superseded an attempted MWERKS_GEKKO asm implementation: prefer C matching tactics over inline assembly outside SDK code.
review_feedback: PsiLupan asked why an empty pragma range was present; the author replied they had just learned what those pragmas meant and would remove it.
PsiLupan caught a standalone #pragma pop with no visible associated push; the author acknowledged with good catch.
PsiLupan later noted a missing #pragma pop after another push, and a dismissed review said the PR should be good once the last pop issue was addressed.
PsiLupan explicitly advised not to use inline assembly, noting it was common before objdiff but is not necessary now outside SDK.
No substantive reviewer feedback on function naming appears; only the PR body mentions dissatisfaction with many temp variable names.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported +5844 matched bytes and 33 new matches, plus 3 broken matches in fn_800DC624, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, and ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolve or discuss the bot-reported broken matches for fn_800DC624, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, and ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll.
Search for follow-up struct-layout work that moves ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs or ftCo_SamusCatchAttrs fields out of local padded structs into shared headers with semantic names.
Look for other reviews by PsiLupan about #pragma push/pop balancing and the no-inline-assembly policy outside SDK code.
Search for later item-scope cleanup around fn_800D7938, fn_800D80F4, ftCo_ItemScopeRapid_Anim, and temp variable renaming.
